title: Research Workflow Example: Archaeology
text: Imagine regional archaeology project recording: sites projectile points ceramic sherd lots survey units places people organizations Heurist, concepts usually modeled record types connected resource pointer fields. heuristR lets inspect structure, query records, perform safe read-modify-write updates R.

title: Research Workflow Example: Archaeology
text: Start creating session logging . local development, usually best keep credentials project .Renviron file rather scripts.
code: library(heuristR)  session <- heurist_session(   base_url = "https://heurist.example.org/heurist",   database = "demo_desert_survey" )  session <- heurist_login(   session,   username = "field_director",   password = Sys.getenv("HEURIST_PASSWORD") )

title: Research Workflow Example: Archaeology
text: writing automation, inspect database structure. archaeology database, might expect record types Site, Projectile point, Survey Unit, Place, fields chronology, material, geometry, site number, linked records.
code: rectypes <- heurist_rectypes(session) fields <- heurist_fields(session) structure <- heurist_structure(session)

title: Research Workflow Example: Archaeology
text: can retrieve single record ID: can also query set records using Heurist’s query syntax: useful reports : sites updated week projectile points linked specific site places within survey zone
code: site_42 <- heurist_get_record(session, 42) late_sites <- heurist_find_records(   session,   q = 't:Site "Late Archaic" sortby:-m' )

title: Research Workflow Example: Archaeology
text: Suppose crew identifies new locality named North Ridge Wash. heurist_create_record() returns heurist_change object. means operation can rolled back later test accidental insert.
code: change_create <- heurist_create_record(   session,   rectype = "Place",   details = list(     "1" = list("0" = "North Ridge Wash")   ) )  new_place_id <- change_create$record_id

title: Research Workflow Example: Archaeology
text: Heurist write endpoints can destructive send incomplete payloads directly. heurist_patch_record() avoids : reading current record merging requested change R sending full replacement payload back Heurist Example: archaeological workflow, safer way add linked Place, correct site title, add newly assigned field identifier preserving unrelated data already stored record.
code: change_patch <- heurist_patch_record(   session,   record_id = 42,   details = list(     "1" = list("0" = "North Ridge Site"),     "1108" = list("0" = new_place_id)   ),   mode = "replace" )

title: Research Workflow Example: Archaeology
text: already know target field record IDs, heurist_link_record() provides clearer helper resource pointers. pattern fits common archaeological relationships : linking projectile point site linking survey unit place linking artifact lot repository collection
code: change_link <- heurist_link_record(   session,   record_id = 108,   field_id = 1108,   target_record_id = 42 )

title: Research Workflow Example: Archaeology
text: Every high-level write helper returns enough information undo change. Rollback especially useful : testing migration script prototyping batch cleanup validating field mappings staging database
code: heurist_rollback(change_patch) heurist_rollback(change_link) heurist_rollback(change_create)

title: Research Workflow Example: Archaeology
text: package also exposes low-level wrappers need raw access: useful debugging building new helpers, high-level functions better default research scripts.
code: raw_read <- heurist_raw_record_output(session, list(q = "t:Site")) raw_edit <- heurist_raw_record_edit(session, list(a = "delete", ids = "9999")) raw_meta <- heurist_raw_entity(session, list(a = "structure", entity = "all"))

title: Research Workflow Example: Archaeology
text: repeatable project work, good pattern : define credentials .Renviron inspect structure heurist_rectypes() heurist_fields() prototype reads heurist_find_records() perform updates heurist_patch_record() keep returned heurist_change objects script validated approach gives much safer path maintaining site, artifact, place data Heurist R.

Bischoff},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000,     https://github.com/Center-for-Archaeology-and-Society/heuristR,     https://heuristnetwork.org/,     https://heurist.huma-num.fr/heurist/startup/},   url = {https://center-for-archaeology-and-society.github.io/heuristR/}, }

title: heuristR
text: heuristR R client Heurist databases session-oriented API. Documentation website: https://center--archaeology--society.github.io/heuristR/

title: heuristR
text: heuristR provides practical R interface working Heurist databases scripts, analysis workflows, repeatable data-management tasks. designed make authentication, metadata inspection, record retrieval, safe scripted updates easier manage R. package especially useful want move beyond manual work Heurist web interface begin building reproducible R workflows : metadata inspection schema exploration record retrieval analysis reporting controlled creation new records safer scripted updates avoid destructive partial saves reversible data maintenance rollback support

title: heuristR
text: Heurist web-based database platform built research projects need flexible, relational data structures without custom application build. public Heurist startup page describes system designed researchers collecting, managing, analysing, visualising, exporting, publishing, archiving information. hosted use, public Huma-Num server currently provides database creation flow heurist.huma-num.fr/heurist/startup/. New users can register create first database server, existing users can create additional databases administration interface logging .

title: heuristR
text: package includes: session authentication helpers metadata access record types, fields, structure low-level wrappers around core Heurist endpoints high-level read helpers safe write helpers built around read-modify-write rollback helpers reversible scripted changes users spend time higher-level helpers heurist_login(), heurist_rectypes(), heurist_find_records(), heurist_get_record(), heurist_create_record(), heurist_patch_record(). low-level wrappers still available need inspect debug raw Heurist payloads.

title: heuristR
text: Install development version GitHub remotes pak, load locally working checkout. GitHub: development local checkout: installation, load package usual:
code: remotes::install_github("Center-for-Archaeology-and-Society/heuristR") devtools::load_all("heuristR") library(heuristR)

title: heuristR
text: easiest public starting point Huma-Num hosted service: Visit heurist.huma-num.fr/heurist/startup/. Register new user, log already account server. Create database name startup form. Open new database begin defining record types fields Design menu. hosted registration workflow referenced verified public startup page April 8, 2026. path changes, consult main Heurist Network website current guidance.

title: heuristR
text: local development live integration tests, create .Renviron file project root. commit .Renviron file file contains live credentials git repository. Example: edit .Renviron values: run R package directory, heuristR’s live tests pick local .Renviron automatically.
code: cp .Renviron.example .Renviron HEURISTR_TEST_BASE_URL=https://your-heurist-host.example/heurist HEURISTR_TEST_DB=your_database_name HEURIST_USERNAME=your_username HEURIST_PASSWORD=your_password

title: heuristR
text: heuristR separates session creation login: heurist_session() creates client object base URL, database name, timeout, cookie storage. heurist_login() uses client authenticate retain returned session cookies. separation keeps configuration distinct authentication makes possible construct session object logging . typical setup looks like : prefer compact mental model, can think “create client, authenticate client.”
code: library(heuristR)  session <- heurist_session(   base_url = Sys.getenv("HEURISTR_TEST_BASE_URL"),   database = Sys.getenv("HEURISTR_TEST_DB") )  session <- heurist_login(   session,   username = Sys.getenv("HEURIST_USERNAME"),   password = Sys.getenv("HEURIST_PASSWORD") )

title: heuristR
text: typical heuristR session looks like : can inspect structure heurist_fields() heurist_structure(), search records heurist_find_records(), use higher-level write helpers need controlled updates. examples illustrative may need adapted Heurist database structure, record types, field IDs, permissions.
code: library(heuristR)  session <- heurist_session(   base_url = Sys.getenv("HEURISTR_TEST_BASE_URL"),   database = Sys.getenv("HEURISTR_TEST_DB") )  session <- heurist_login(   session,   username = Sys.getenv("HEURIST_USERNAME"),   password = Sys.getenv("HEURIST_PASSWORD") )  rectypes <- heurist_rectypes(session) record <- heurist_get_record(session, 3)

title: heuristR
text: writing automation Heurist database, usually helpful inspect available record types field definitions. Typical use cases include: finding exact name record type Place Site locating numeric field ID pointer field title field understanding project database models people, places, objects, events need direct access underlying structure response, use lower-level metadata wrapper:
code: rectypes <- heurist_rectypes(session) fields <- heurist_fields(session) structure <- heurist_structure(session) raw_structure <- heurist_raw_entity(   session,   list(a = "structure", entity = "all") )

title: heuristR
text: two common read patterns: fetch known record ID, search set records. Fetch single record: Search records using Heurist query syntax: Another example using fictional thematic query: want raw endpoint response rather higher-level parsed helper:
code: record <- heurist_get_record(session, 3) sites <- heurist_find_records(   session,   q = "t:Site sortby:-m" ) late_sites <- heurist_find_records(   session,   q = 't:Site "Late Archaic" sortby:-m' ) raw_records <- heurist_raw_record_output(   session,   list(q = "t:Site", format = "json") )

title: heuristR
text: New records can created heurist_create_record(). example, following illustrative example creates fictional Place record: returned object heurist_change, includes enough information support rollback later needed.
code: change_create <- heurist_create_record(   session,   rectype = "Place",   details = list(     "1" = list("0" = "North Ridge Wash")   ) ) new_record_id <- change_create$record_id

title: heuristR
text: Direct Heurist save operations can destructive send incomplete payloads. heuristR avoids using read-modify-write approach heurist_patch_record(). Example: helper first fetches existing record, merges requested change R, submits full replacement payload back Heurist.
code: change_patch <- heurist_patch_record(   session,   record_id = 42,   details = list(     "1" = list("0" = "Updated Site Title")   ),   mode = "replace" )

title: heuristR
text: heuristR avoids partial destructive saves default. High-level helpers heurist_patch_record() fetch current record, merge requested changes client-side, send back full record payload. write returns heurist_change object can rolled back heurist_restore_change() heurist_rollback(). need full explicit replacement rather merge-style patch, use heurist_replace_record().
code: change <- heurist_patch_record(   session,   record_id = 3,   details = list("1" = list("0" = "Updated title")),   mode = "replace" )  heurist_rollback(change) change_replace <- heurist_replace_record(   session,   record_id = 42,   details = list(     "1" = list("0" = "Replacement title")   ) )

title: heuristR
text: Heurist databases often use resource pointer fields connect records. case, heurist_link_record() provides clearer workflow manually constructing detail payloads . pattern useful examples : linking object place linking person organization linking site locality region record
code: change_link <- heurist_link_record(   session,   record_id = 108,   field_id = 1108,   target_record_id = 42 )

title: heuristR
text: One useful features heuristR high-level write helpers return change objects can undone. especially helpful : testing script staging database validating field mappings prototyping cleanup migration workflow making scripted edits may need reverse quickly
code: heurist_rollback(change_create) heurist_rollback(change_patch) heurist_rollback(change_link)

title: heuristR
text: need raw access underlying Heurist controllers, package also provides low-level wrappers. helpful : debugging request payloads inspecting server responses directly prototyping new higher-level helpers
code: raw_read <- heurist_raw_record_output(session, list(q = "t:Site")) raw_edit <- heurist_raw_record_edit(session, list(a = "delete", ids = "9999")) raw_meta <- heurist_raw_entity(session, list(a = "structure", entity = "all"))

title: heuristR
text: Additional package documentation available : GitHub Pages site: https://center--archaeology--society.github.io/heuristR/ Function reference: https://center--archaeology--society.github.io/heuristR/reference/ Workflow vignette: https://center--archaeology--society.github.io/heuristR/articles/archaeology-workflow.html

title: heuristR
text: package includes permanent unit live integration tests covering: auth/session metadata raw endpoint wrappers reads safe create/replace/patch flows link helpers rollback behavior

what: heuristR: Session-Oriented Access to Heurist — heuristR-package
title: heuristR: Session-Oriented Access to Heurist — heuristR-package
text: heuristR provides session-oriented wrapper around Heurist authentication, metadata, record retrieval endpoints. package designed support safe client-side merge workflows record updates, rather relying destructive partial save calls.

title: heuristR: Session-Oriented Access to Heurist — heuristR-package
text: Maintainer: Robert J. Bischoff rbischoff@asu.edu

text: Creates new record using Heurist record type ID details payload.

code: heurist_create_record(   session,   rectype_id,   details,   title = NULL,   owner_ugrp_id = NULL,   non_owner_visibility = NULL )

what: Patch a Heurist Record Safely — heurist_patch_record
title: Patch a Heurist Record Safely — heurist_patch_record
text: Performs safe read-modify-write update. Existing details fetched, normalized, merged supplied changes, written back full record payload avoid accidental data loss.

title: Patch a Heurist Record Safely — heurist_patch_record
code: heurist_patch_record(   session,   record_id,   details,   mode = c("replace", "append") )

title: Patch a Heurist Record Safely — heurist_patch_record
text: session heurist_session. record_id Record ID. details Named list detail changes. mode Merge mode: "replace" "append".

title: Patch a Heurist Record Safely — heurist_patch_record
text: heurist_change object.
